intro_templates: It's an apple! Apples are yummy fruits.
Look, an apple! It is round and shiny.
A red apple! Apples grow on trees.
Hello, apple! Can you see its stem?
Yum, an apple! It is good to eat.

facts.text: Apples are round and can be red, green or yellow.
Apples grow on trees in orchards.
Apples are crunchy and juicy when you bite them.
Inside an apple are small brown seeds.
Apples are a healthy snack.

questions.text: What color is this apple?
What shape is an apple?
Where do apples grow?
How does an apple feel when you bite it?
What is inside an apple?
questions.hint: Small brown things in the middle.

intro_templates: It's an apple! Did you know apples float in water?
Look at this apple! There are many different types of apples.
Here's an apple! Apples can be made into juice and sauce.
Check out this apple! It has a star shape inside if you cut it across!
Meet this apple! Apples help keep our bodies healthy.

facts.text: If you put an apple in water, it will float because it is less dense.
There are over 7,500 different kinds of apples in the world.
Apples can be made into apple juice, applesauce, and apple pie.
Eating apples helps keep our teeth clean and our bodies strong.

questions.text: What happens if you put an apple in water?
questions.hint: It doesn't sink like a rock!

questions.text: Are there many kinds of apples?
What can we make from apples?
Why are apples good for our body?
questions.hint: They help clean teeth and give energy.

intro_templates: It's an apple! Did you know an apple tree needs bees to make fruit?
Look at this apple! The tiny seeds inside can grow into a whole new tree.
This is an apple. Once picked, it continues to ripen and can change color.
Here we have an apple. Its skin has a special wax to keep it fresh.
Consider this apple. Without bees visiting apple flowers, we might have no apples.

facts.text: Apple trees need bees to carry pollen from flower to flower to make apples grow.
Each apple seed can grow into a completely new apple tree, different from its parent.
After picking, apples continue to ripen and may change color slightly due to a gas they release.
Apple skin has a natural wax coating that helps keep moisture in and protects the fruit.
Apples are part of the rose family, which means they are related to roses and pears.

questions.text: How do bees help apple trees make apples?
questions.hint: They move yellow powder between flowers.

questions.text: What can grow from an apple seed?
questions.hint: It starts as a small plant.

questions.text: What happens to an apple after it is picked from the tree?
questions.hint: It can get softer and sweeter.

questions.text: Why does an apple's skin feel a bit waxy?
questions.hint: It helps the apple stay juicy.

questions.text: What other plants are apples related to?

intro_templates: It's a banana! A yellow fruit. Can you find a banana?
Look, a banana! It is long and curved. What color is it?
A tasty banana! It grows on a plant. Do you like bananas?
Hello, banana! Let's peel it. What's inside?
Yum, a banana! It is soft and sweet. Is it ready to eat?

facts.text: Bananas are long, curved, and yellow.
Bananas have a peel you remove to eat.
Bananas are soft and sweet inside.
Bananas grow in bunches on tall plants.
Bananas are a healthy snack for kids.

questions.text: What color is a ripe banana?
What shape is a banana?
How many bananas are in this bunch?
Do we eat the banana peel?
Is a banana a fruit or a vegetable?

intro_templates: It's a banana! Do you know that bananas come from very tall plants, not trees?
Look at this banana! When it gets brown spots, it becomes even sweeter.
Here's a banana! Bananas are packed with energy and good for our muscles.
Check out this banana! Bananas don't grow in cold places; they love warm weather.
Meet this banana! Some bananas are green, and some are yellow. Why is that?

facts.text: Bananas start out green and turn yellow as they ripen.
Bananas give us quick energy because they have natural sugars.
A banana plant is the world's largest herb, not a real tree.
Brown spots on a banana mean it is very ripe and extra sweet.
You can use mashed bananas to make yummy banana bread.

questions.text: What color is an unripe banana?
Why are bananas a good snack before playing?
Is a banana plant a tree or a giant herb?
What do brown spots on a banana mean?
What can you make with very ripe, spotty bananas?

intro_templates: It's a banana! Did you know that all the bananas we eat are clones of each other?
Look at this banana! It has tiny seeds inside, but they are too small to grow new plants.
This is a banana. People have been eating them for thousands of years, all around the world.
Here we have a banana. They are picked green and travel long distances to reach our stores.
Consider this banana. It can help our bodies in many ways, from digestion to mood.

facts.text: Almost all bananas we buy in stores are from the same type of plant, called Cavendish.
Bananas are picked while still green so they don't spoil during shipping to stores.
Bananas contain a special fiber that helps our digestive system work smoothly.
Wild bananas have many big, hard seeds, but the bananas we eat have tiny seeds.
If you put a banana next to other fruit, it can help them ripen faster.

questions.text: Why are most bananas in stores the same shape and taste?
questions.hint: They come from the same kind of plant!

questions.text: Why are bananas picked when they are green instead of yellow?
questions.hint: Think about traveling on a truck for days!

questions.text: How can eating a banana help your tummy feel better?
questions.hint: It has a special kind of fiber!

questions.text: How are wild bananas different from the ones we eat?
What happens if you put a banana in a bag with green avocados?
questions.hint: Bananas give off a special gas!

visual_features.size: medium (about the length of a hand)

intro_templates: It's a carrot! Carrots are orange and crunchy.
Look, a carrot! Rabbits love to eat carrots.
A tasty carrot! It grows under the ground.
Hello, carrot! It has a green leafy top.
Yum, a carrot! It is good for your eyes.

facts.text: Carrots are usually bright orange.
Carrots are crunchy when you bite them.
Carrots grow under the ground.
Rabbits love to eat carrots.
You can eat carrots raw or cooked.

questions.text: What color is a carrot usually?
How does a carrot feel when you bite it?
Where do carrots grow?
What animal likes to eat carrots?
Can you eat a carrot without cooking it?

intro_templates: It's a carrot! Did you know carrots help you see better in the dark?
Look at this carrot! The part we eat is actually the root of the plant.
Here's a carrot! Carrots come in different colors like purple and yellow.
Check out this carrot! It takes about two to three months for a carrot to grow.
Meet this carrot! You can plant the green top, but it won't grow a new carrot.

facts.text: Carrots contain vitamin A, which helps your eyes stay healthy.
The carrot we eat is the root of the carrot plant.
Carrots can be purple, yellow, white, or red, not just orange.
It takes about 70 to 80 days for a carrot to grow from seed.
Baby carrots are not baby plants, they are cut from bigger carrots.

questions.text: What vitamin in carrots helps your eyes?
What part of the plant is the carrot we eat?
Name one color of carrot besides orange.
How long does it take for a carrot to grow from seed?
Are baby carrots really baby plants?
questions.hint: They are cut from big carrots!

intro_templates: It's a carrot! Did you know that eating too many carrots can make your skin turn slightly orange?
Look at this carrot! The green tops of carrots are not usually eaten because they can taste bitter.
Here's a carrot! Long ago, carrots were not orange; they were purple or yellow.
Check out this carrot! Carrots store energy in their roots to help the plant survive the winter.
Meet this carrot! If you plant a carrot top in water, it will grow new green leaves but not a new carrot.

facts.text: If you eat too many carrots, your skin might turn slightly orange, but it's harmless.
The green leafy tops of carrots are not usually eaten because they can taste bitter.
Originally, carrots were purple or yellow; orange carrots were developed later by farmers.
Carrots store sugar in their roots, which is why they taste sweet.
You can grow new green leaves from a carrot top in water, but it won't grow a new edible carrot.

questions.text: What changes will occur to the skin if one eats too many carrots?
Why don't people usually eat the green tops of carrots?
What color were carrots originally, long ago?
Why do carrots taste sweet?
If you put a carrot top in water, what will grow?

intro_templates: Look, an egg! It comes from a chicken.
It's an egg! Eggs can be white or brown.
Hello, egg! Eggs are a good food. Are they big or small?
We have an egg! Let's learn about it. Can you see its shape?
Wow, an egg! I like eggs. Do you like eggs?

facts.text: An egg has a hard shell outside.
Eggs are oval, not round like a ball.
Eggs come from a chicken mom.
We can boil, fry, or scramble an egg.
An egg has two parts: the white and the yellow yolk.

questions.text: What animal do eggs come from?
What is the shape of an egg?
What color is this egg's shell?
What is the yellow part inside called?
Can we eat eggs for breakfast?
questions.hint: They are a common breakfast food!

intro_templates: It's an egg! Eggs are full of good things that help our bodies grow strong.
Look at this egg! Did you know a raw egg and a boiled egg are very different inside?
Here's an egg! Eggs are used to make many foods, like cakes and pancakes.
Check out this egg! A raw egg is liquid, but heat makes it turn solid.
Meet this food, an egg! Some parts we eat are from animals, and eggs are one of them.

facts.text: Eggs are very healthy and help our muscles and bones grow strong.
The clear part of a raw egg is called the egg white, and it turns white when cooked.
Eggs are a key ingredient in baking, helping cakes rise and become fluffy.
A fresh egg sinks in water, but an old egg will float to the top.
A raw egg will not spin fast, but a hard-boiled egg will spin smoothly.

questions.text: Why are eggs good food for our body?
What happens to the clear egg white when we cook it?
What can we use eggs for besides eating them alone?
How can we test if an egg is fresh with a glass of water?
questions.hint: See if it sinks or floats!

questions.text: Which spins more stable, a raw egg or a boiled egg?

intro_templates: It's an egg! The shell has tiny holes you cannot see, which let air in and out.
Look at this egg! It is designed by nature to protect the baby chick growing inside.
Here's an egg. The yolk provides food for a baby chick, and the white protects it.
This food is an egg. Most eggs we eat will not hatch, because they are not fertilized.
Consider this egg. Its strong oval shape can handle pressure from the outside without breaking easily.

facts.text: An eggshell has thousands of tiny holes that allow air to pass through for a chick to breathe.
The yolk is the food supply for a baby chick, full of vitamins and fat to help it grow.
The eggs we buy in stores usually cannot become chicks because they were not fertilized by a rooster.
The oval shape of an egg is very strong, distributing pressure evenly so it does not crack easily.
If you shake a fresh egg near your ear, you will not hear much, but an old egg might make a sloshing sound.

questions.text: Why does an eggshell have tiny holes that we cannot see?
questions.hint: Think about what living things need.

questions.text: What is the main job of the egg yolk for a baby chick?
questions.expected_answer: to feed it / be its food

questions.text: Can a store-bought egg ever hatch into a baby chick?
questions.hint: It needs a mom and a dad chicken.

questions.text: Why is an egg's oval shape a good design?
questions.hint: It makes it strong against squeezing.

questions.text: If you hear a sloshing sound when shaking an egg, what does it probably mean?

visual_features.size: varies (from small roll to large loaf)

intro_templates: It's bread! We eat bread every day. What color is it?
Look, bread! It is soft inside and crusty outside.
A loaf of bread! We can slice it to make toast.
Hello, bread! It has a nice smell. Can you smell it?
Wow, bread! We eat bread with jam or butter.

facts.text: Bread is soft on the inside and crusty on the outside.
Bread can be white, brown, or golden in color.
We slice bread to make sandwiches and toast.
Bread comes in different shapes like loaves and rolls.
Bread smells good when it is fresh from the oven.

questions.text: What is the outside of bread called?
How do we usually cut bread?
questions.hint: We use a knife to make flat pieces!

questions.text: What color is this bread?
Is bread soft inside or outside?
Can we make toast with bread?
questions.hint: We put it in a toaster!

intro_templates: It's bread! Bread is made from simple ingredients like flour and water.
Look at this bread! Did you know it has tiny air holes that make it fluffy?
Here's bread! It is a staple food in many countries around the world.
Check out this bread! Different types of bread come from different cultures.
Meet this bread! It provides us with energy to play and learn all day.

facts.text: Bread is made from flour, water, yeast, and salt mixed together.
The tiny holes inside bread are made by yeast bubbles when it rises.
Bread is a good source of carbohydrates that give our bodies energy.
Different cultures have different breads like baguettes, naan, and tortillas.
Bread needs to be baked in a hot oven to become the food we eat.

questions.text: What makes bread rise and become fluffy?
questions.hint: It's a tiny living thing that makes bubbles!

questions.text: What are the main ingredients in bread?
Why is bread good for giving us energy?
questions.hint: It has carbohydrates that fuel our bodies!

questions.text: Can you name one type of bread from another country?
questions.hint: Like a long, thin French bread!

questions.text: How is bread cooked?
questions.hint: It goes into a hot box to get brown!

intro_templates: It's bread! Did you know that bread has been made by humans for over 10,000 years?
Look at this bread! The process of making bread involves fermentation, which is like magic.
This is bread. Even though it seems simple, it's the result of careful science and timing.
Here we have bread. It can go stale or moldy if we don't store it properly.
Consider this bread. It can be used in many creative ways beyond just sandwiches.

facts.text: Bread was one of the first foods ever made by humans, starting over 10,000 years ago.
Yeast eats sugar in the dough and produces gas, which makes the bread rise and become airy.
Bread goes stale when it loses moisture, but toasting it can make it crispy again.
Mold can grow on bread if it is kept in a damp and warm place for too long.
Bread can be used to make breadcrumbs, croutons, and even bread pudding to avoid waste.

questions.text: How long have humans been making bread?
What does yeast do to make bread rise?
questions.hint: It eats sugar and makes gas!

questions.text: What happens to bread when it goes stale?
Why does mold grow on old bread?
questions.hint: Tiny living things like damp places!

questions.text: What can we make with old bread so we don't waste it?
questions.hint: Think about crunchy toppings or a dessert!

intro_templates: It's milk! Milk is a white drink.
Look, milk! It comes from cows.
A glass of milk! Milk is good for you.
Hello, milk! It is cold and yummy.
Yum, milk! We drink it with cookies.

facts.text: Milk is white like snow.
Milk comes from cows on a farm.
We drink milk from a glass.
Milk helps make your bones strong.

questions.text: What color is milk?
Where does milk come from?
How do we drink milk?
questions.hint: We use a cup or glass!

questions.text: Does milk help your bones?

intro_templates: It's milk! Do you know milk has a special sugar called lactose?
Look at this milk! It has many vitamins that help you grow tall and strong.
Here's some milk! You can make many different foods from milk, like cheese and yogurt.
Check out this milk! Not everyone can drink milk from cows; some people drink milk from plants.
Meet this milk! It's not just a drink; it's used in cooking many delicious foods.

facts.text: Milk contains calcium, which helps build strong bones and teeth.
You can make cheese, yogurt, and butter from milk.
Some people drink milk from plants, like soy milk or almond milk.
Milk is kept cold in the fridge so it stays fresh and good to drink.
Baby cows drink their mother's milk, just like human babies drink milk.

questions.text: What mineral in milk helps your bones?
Name one food we can make from milk.
Where should we keep milk so it doesn't spoil?
What is plant milk made from?
Do baby animals drink milk like human babies?

intro_templates: It's milk! Did you know that milk needs to be pasteurized to make it safe to drink?
Look at this milk! The white color comes from tiny fat and protein particles that reflect light.
Here's some milk! Milk is an emulsion, which means fat droplets are suspended in water.
Consider this milk. When milk gets old, bacteria make it sour and lumpy, turning it into something new.
Think about milk. If we didn't have refrigeration, how would people keep milk from spoiling long ago?

facts.text: Milk is heated in a process called pasteurization to kill harmful germs and make it safe.
Milk looks white because tiny fat and protein particles scatter light in all directions.
When milk sours, bacteria turn the lactose sugar into lactic acid, making it taste tangy.
People long ago made milk into cheese or yogurt to preserve it without refrigeration.
If you shake heavy cream for a long time, the fat clumps together to make butter.

questions.text: Why is milk heated before we buy it?
Why does milk appear white in color?
What makes milk taste sour when it goes bad?
How did people keep milk from spoiling before refrigerators?
questions.hint: They turned it into other foods!

questions.text: What happens when you shake heavy cream for a long time?

intro_templates: It's a strawberry! It's red and sweet.
Look, a strawberry! See the green leaves on top?
A yummy strawberry! It has tiny seeds on the outside.
Hello, strawberry! Let's look at this pretty fruit.
Yum, a strawberry! Can you see its heart shape?

facts.text: Strawberries are bright red when ripe.
Strawberries have a sweet and juicy taste.
Strawberries grow on low plants near the ground.
Strawberries have tiny yellow seeds on the outside.
You eat the red part, not the green leaves.

questions.text: What color is a ripe strawberry?
How does a strawberry taste?
Where do strawberries grow?
questions.hint: They grow close to the ground!

questions.text: Where are the seeds on a strawberry?
Do we eat the green leaves on a strawberry?

intro_templates: It's a strawberry! Did you know strawberries are not really berries?
Look at this strawberry! Its seeds grow on the outside, which is unusual for fruits.
Here's a strawberry! Strawberries belong to the rose family, just like roses!
Check out this strawberry! It's packed with Vitamin C, which keeps us healthy.
Meet this strawberry! It's one of the few fruits with seeds on the outside.

facts.text: Strawberries are the only fruit with seeds on the outside.
Strawberries are packed with Vitamin C, which helps keep us healthy.
Each strawberry has about 200 tiny seeds on its surface.
Strawberries are not true berries, but bananas and tomatoes are.
Strawberries can be made into jam, which is cooked fruit with sugar.

questions.text: What is special about where strawberry seeds grow?
What vitamin in strawberries helps keep us healthy?
How many seeds does one strawberry have about?
questions.hint: It's a lot of tiny seeds!

questions.text: Are strawberries true berries like bananas?
What can we make from strawberries and sugar?

intro_templates: It's a strawberry! Did you know strawberries need bees to help them grow properly?
Look at this strawberry! The red color is actually a signal to animals that it's ready to eat.
Here's a strawberry! Each of those tiny seeds could grow into a whole new plant.
Check out this strawberry! They are called 'straw' berries because farmers used straw to protect them.
Meet this strawberry! They're one of the first fruits to ripen in springtime.

facts.text: Bees help strawberries grow by moving pollen from flower to flower.
The red color signals to birds and animals that the strawberry is ripe and sweet.
Each tiny seed on a strawberry can grow into a whole new strawberry plant.
Farmers used to put straw around strawberry plants to protect the fruit, hence the name.
Strawberries are among the first fruits to ripen in spring, bringing early sweetness.

questions.text: How do bees help strawberries grow?
questions.hint: They help the flowers make fruit!

questions.text: Why are strawberries red when they're ripe?
What can grow from each tiny seed on a strawberry?
questions.hint: It could grow into a big plant!

questions.text: Why are they called 'straw' berries?
questions.hint: Farmers used it to protect them!

questions.text: When do strawberries usually ripen?
questions.hint: It's one of the first spring fruits!

intro_templates: This is rice! Rice is a yummy food. Can you see the small white grains?
Look, rice! It is soft and fluffy. Do you like to eat rice?
Here is some rice! Rice is often served in a bowl. Is it hot or cold?
Hello, rice! Rice comes in small grains. Can you count some grains?
Yum, rice! Rice is a common food. What color is it?

facts.text: Rice is small, white grains that we eat.
Cooked rice is soft and fluffy to eat.
Rice is often served hot in a bowl.
We eat rice with chopsticks or a spoon.
Rice grows in fields with lots of water.

questions.text: What color is cooked white rice?
questions.hint: Look at it, it's light colored!

questions.text: Is cooked rice soft or hard?
How do we usually eat rice?
questions.hint: We use tools to pick it up!

questions.text: Can you count to three rice grains?
questions.hint: One grain, two grains, three grains!

questions.text: Where does rice grow?

intro_templates: This is rice! Did you know that rice is a staple food for many people around the world?
Look at this rice! Before it is cooked, rice is hard and cannot be eaten.
Here's rice! Rice can be different colors, like white, brown, or even black.
Check out this rice! Rice is often eaten with vegetables, meat, or fish.
Meet rice! Rice plants grow in special fields called paddies that are filled with water.

facts.text: Rice is a staple food for more than half of the world's population.
Uncooked rice grains are hard and must be cooked in water to become soft.
Brown rice is healthier than white rice because it has more nutrients.
Rice is often eaten with other foods like vegetables, meat, or fish.
Rice grows in special fields called paddies that are flooded with water.

questions.text: Why do we need to cook rice before eating it?
questions.hint: Raw rice is too hard to chew!

questions.text: What is the difference between white rice and brown rice?
questions.hint: One has more nutrients and is darker!

questions.text: What do we often eat with rice?
questions.hint: Foods that make a meal complete!

questions.text: What are the fields where rice grows called?
questions.hint: They are very wet and muddy!

questions.text: How many people in the world eat rice as a main food?
questions.hint: More than half of all people!

intro_templates: This is rice! Did you know that rice plants need a lot of water and sunshine to grow?
Look at this rice! Rice is a type of grass seed that people have been eating for thousands of years.
Here's rice! When rice is cooked, it absorbs water and becomes much larger and softer.
Check out this rice! Rice is so important that in some cultures, it is a symbol of life and good luck.
Meet rice! Rice can be used to make many different foods, like rice cakes, rice noodles, and even rice milk.

facts.text: Rice plants need plenty of water, sunshine, and warm weather to grow well.
Rice is actually the seed of a type of grass plant that grows in water.
When rice is cooked, it absorbs water and swells to become much larger and softer.
In some countries, rice is thrown at weddings for good luck and to wish a happy life.
Rice can be made into many foods like rice cakes, rice noodles, rice milk, and even rice vinegar.

questions.text: What does a rice plant need to grow healthy and strong?
questions.hint: Think about water, sun, and warmth!

questions.text: What part of the rice plant do we actually eat?
questions.hint: It's the part that can grow into a new plant!

questions.text: Why does rice become bigger and softer when we cook it?
questions.hint: It soaks up the water like a sponge!

questions.text: Why do people sometimes throw rice at weddings?
questions.hint: It is a tradition for good luck!

questions.text: What are some other foods we can make from rice besides plain rice?
questions.hint: Think of snacks, drinks, and more!

intro_templates: These are noodles! They are long and thin. Can you see how long they are?
Look, noodles! They come in different shapes. What color are these?
Yummy noodles! We eat noodles with a fork or chopsticks. How do you eat them?
Hello, noodles! They are soft and chewy. Do you like noodles?
Wow, noodles! They can be straight or curly. Which shape do you like best?

facts.text: Noodles are long, thin strips of food.
Noodles can be straight, curly, or shaped like tubes.
We eat noodles with chopsticks, forks, or spoons.
Noodles are soft and chewy when they are cooked.
Some noodles are white, and some are yellow.

questions.text: What shape are most noodles?
How do we usually eat noodles?
questions.hint: We use tools to pick them up!

questions.text: Are cooked noodles soft or hard?
What color are these noodles?
Can noodles be curly?

intro_templates: These are noodles! They are made from flour and water. Did you know they come from many countries?
Look at these noodles! Different shapes have different names, like spaghetti or ramen.
Here are noodles! They need to be cooked in boiling water before we can eat them.
Check out these noodles! Some are made with eggs, which makes them yellow and tasty.
Meet these noodles! They can be served in soup, with sauce, or stir-fried in a pan.

facts.text: Noodles are made from flour mixed with water or eggs.
Noodles need to be cooked in hot water until they become soft.
Different countries have their own special noodles, like Italian pasta or Asian ramen.
Noodles can be served with soup, meat, vegetables, or cheese sauce.
Noodles give us energy because they are made from grains like wheat or rice.

questions.text: What are the two main ingredients to make noodles?
How do we cook dry noodles to make them edible?
questions.hint: We use a pot and hot water!

questions.text: Can you name one type of noodle from another country?
Why do noodles give us energy?
questions.expected_answer: because they are made from grains

questions.text: What can we eat together with noodles?
questions.hint: They are often mixed with other foods!

intro_templates: These are noodles! Did you know that noodles were invented in China over 4,000 years ago?
Look at these noodles! The way they are dried or fresh affects how they are cooked.
Here are noodles! Some noodles are made from rice or beans, not just wheat flour.
Check out these noodles! The shape of the noodle can help it hold sauce better.
Consider these noodles! Noodles can be preserved by drying, which lets us store them for a long time.

facts.text: Noodles were first made in ancient China and are one of the oldest prepared foods.
Dried noodles can be stored for months, but fresh noodles need to be eaten quickly.
Some noodles are made from rice or mung beans, which makes them without wheat.
Ridged or hollow noodles like penne can hold more sauce inside them.
If you stretch a piece of noodle dough very thin, it can become as thin as a thread.

questions.text: Which ancient country first invented noodles?
Why can dried noodles last longer than fresh noodles?
What are two alternative ingredients to make noodles besides wheat?
How does a hollow noodle shape help when eating with sauce?
questions.hint: It can trap the sauce inside!

questions.text: What might happen if you cook noodles for too long?

intro_templates: It's fish! Fish is good to eat. Look at its color!
Yummy fish! Fish is a healthy food. Can you see the flakes?
We eat fish! Fish comes from the water. Is it pink or white?
Hello, fish! This food is good for you. How many pieces do you see?
Tasty fish! Fish can be cooked in many ways. What shape is it?

facts.text: Fish is a healthy food that comes from water.
Fish can be pink, white, or orange in color.
Fish has soft, flaky meat when cooked.
We eat fish to help our bodies grow strong.
Fish can be baked, fried, or grilled.

questions.text: What color is this fish?
Where does fish come from?
How does cooked fish feel?
Why do we eat fish?
Can we bake fish?

intro_templates: It's fish! Fish is full of good things for our body. Do you know it helps our brain?
Look at this fish! Fish oil is very healthy and helps our hearts stay strong.
Here's fish! Fish is a great source of protein, which builds our muscles.
Check out this fish! Some fish have bones, so we must eat carefully.
Meet this fish! Fish can be eaten in many ways, like in sushi or fish sticks.

facts.text: Fish contains special oils that are very good for our heart and brain.
Fish is an excellent source of protein, which helps our muscles grow.
Some fish, like salmon, have tiny bones that we need to remove before eating.
Fish can be eaten raw in sushi, or cooked in many different ways.
Eating fish regularly can help improve our memory and concentration.

questions.text: What part of our body does fish oil help?
What nutrient in fish helps muscles grow?
Why do we need to be careful when eating some fish?
questions.hint: They can be small and sharp!

questions.text: Name two ways we can eat fish.
How does eating fish help us at school?
questions.hint: It helps us think and remember!

intro_templates: It's fish! Did you know that fish is one of the healthiest foods in the world?
Look at this fish! The way we cook fish can change its nutrients and taste.
This is fish. Some people are allergic to fish, so they must avoid it.
Here we have fish. Fish populations need to be protected so we can keep eating them.
Consider this fish. If we didn't have fish, what other foods could give us the same benefits?

facts.text: Fish contains Omega-3 fatty acids, which are essential for brain development and health.
Cooking methods like steaming or baking preserve more of fish's healthy nutrients than frying.
Some people have fish allergies, which means their body reacts badly to eating fish.
Overfishing can reduce fish populations, so we need to choose sustainably caught fish.
If you don't eat fish, you can get similar nutrients from foods like walnuts, flaxseeds, or supplements.

questions.text: What special nutrient in fish helps our brains?
questions.hint: It's a type of healthy fat!

questions.text: Which cooking method can keep the fish in its most nutritious state?
Why can't some people eat fish?
Why is it important to protect fish populations?
questions.hint: So we can keep eating fish in the future!

questions.text: What are some alternatives if someone doesn't eat fish?

intro_templates: It's a watermelon! Look at its green skin!
Wow, a watermelon! It is big and round. Can you see the stripes?
A juicy watermelon! It is red inside with black seeds.
Hello, watermelon! Let's tap it to hear a thump sound.
Yum, watermelon! It is sweet and juicy.

facts.text: Watermelons have green skin with stripes.
Watermelons are red inside with black seeds.
Watermelons taste sweet and juicy.
Watermelons grow on vines on the ground.
Watermelons are very heavy to carry.

questions.text: What color is watermelon skin?
How many seeds can you count in a slice?
What color is watermelon inside?
How does watermelon taste?
Is watermelon light or heavy?

intro_templates: It's a watermelon! Do you know it is 92% water? That's why it's so juicy!
Look at this watermelon! It grows from a tiny seed into a big fruit.
Here's a watermelon! Did you know some watermelons have no seeds at all?
Check out this watermelon! It's perfect for hot days because it helps keep us hydrated.
Meet this watermelon! We eat the juicy part, but the rind can be pickled or cooked too.

facts.text: Watermelon is 92% water, which helps keep our bodies hydrated on hot days.
Some watermelons are seedless because farmers developed special varieties.
You can tell if a watermelon is ripe by tapping it; a hollow thump means it's ready.
Watermelon seeds can be roasted and eaten as a crunchy snack.
Watermelons grow from yellow flowers that turn into small green fruits.

questions.text: Why is watermelon so juicy?
questions.expected_answer: it has a lot of water

questions.text: Are all watermelons full of black seeds?
How can you tell if a watermelon is ripe?
questions.expected_answer: by tapping it for a hollow sound

questions.text: What part of the watermelon can be roasted and eaten?
What color are watermelon flowers?

intro_templates: It's a watermelon! Did you know it's actually considered a vegetable by botanists because it's related to cucumbers?
Look at this watermelon! Its high water content not only hydrates but also helps cool our body temperature.
This is a watermelon. Seedless watermelons aren't completely seedless; they have soft, white edible seeds.
Here we have a watermelon. Farmers plant them in warm soil because they need lots of sun and space to grow.
Consider this watermelon. The striped pattern on the rind helps protect it from too much sun exposure.

facts.text: Watermelon is botanically classified as a vegetable because it belongs to the cucumber and squash family.
The stripes on a watermelon's rind help to regulate its temperature and protect it from sunburn.
Seedless watermelons are grown from seeds that produce fruits with soft, white, edible seed coats.
Eating watermelon can help cool your body because it contains so much water and nutrients.
Watermelons need about 3-4 months of warm weather to grow from seed to harvest.

questions.text: Is watermelon a fruit or a vegetable to scientists?
Why do watermelons have stripes on their rind?
questions.hint: Think about protection from the sun!

questions.text: How are seedless watermelons different from regular ones?
questions.expected_answer: they have soft white seeds instead of hard black ones

questions.text: How does eating watermelon help on a hot day?
What kind of weather do watermelons need to grow?

visual_features.size: varies (small cupcake to big birthday cake)

intro_templates: It's a cake! Cakes are sweet and yummy!
Look, a cake! It has frosting on top. What color is it?
A birthday cake! It has candles to blow out.
Yum, cake! Is it big or small?
Hello, cake! Let's count the candles on it!

facts.text: Cakes are sweet treats we eat for dessert.
Cakes often have colorful frosting on top.
Birthday cakes have candles to blow out.
Cakes can be round, square, or other shapes.
Cakes are soft and fluffy to eat.

questions.text: What is on top of many cakes?
How many candles are on this birthday cake?
What do we do with candles on a birthday cake?
What color is the frosting on this cake?
Is cake sweet or sour?

intro_templates: It's a cake! Cakes are made by baking batter in an oven.
Look at this cake! Did you know cakes need special ingredients to rise?
Here's a cake! Different types of cake have different flavors, like chocolate or vanilla.
Check out this cake! The frosting is made from sugar and butter.
Meet this cake! Cakes are often served at celebrations like birthdays and weddings.

facts.text: Cakes are baked in a hot oven to make them rise and become fluffy.
Cakes need baking powder or baking soda to help them rise in the oven.
Different cakes have different flavors, like chocolate, vanilla, or strawberry.
Frosting is a sweet topping made from sugar, butter, and sometimes milk.
We often eat cakes at special times like birthdays, weddings, and holidays.

questions.text: Where do we bake a cake to make it cook?
What ingredient helps a cake rise and become fluffy?
Name two different flavors of cake.
What is frosting made from?
When do people often eat cake?

intro_templates: It's a cake! Did you know that the air bubbles in cake batter make it light and fluffy when baked?
Look at this cake! The frosting acts as a seal to keep the cake moist and fresh.
This is a cake. When we mix ingredients, we have to be careful not to overmix the batter.
Here we have a cake. The reason we add eggs to cake batter is to help hold everything together.
Consider this cake. If you baked a cake at too high a temperature, what do you think would happen?

facts.text: Tiny air bubbles in the cake batter expand in the oven's heat, making the cake light and fluffy.
Frosting helps to keep the cake moist by sealing in the moisture and preventing it from drying out.
If you mix cake batter too much, the cake can become tough instead of soft and tender.
Eggs in cake batter act like glue, helping to bind all the other ingredients together when baked.
If you bake a cake at too high a temperature, the outside might burn before the inside is fully cooked.

questions.text: What makes a cake light and fluffy when it bakes in the oven?
questions.hint: Think about what's inside the batter!

questions.text: How does frosting help keep a cake fresh?
What might happen if you mix cake batter for too long?
Why do we put eggs in cake batter?
questions.hint: They help hold it all together!

questions.text: What problem occurs if the oven is too hot when baking a cake?

visual_features.size: varies (from small scoop to large tub)

intro_templates: It's ice cream! Ice cream is cold and sweet.
Look, ice cream! It comes in many colors. What color is this?
Yummy ice cream! Ice cream melts in the sun.
Hello, ice cream! Let's count the scoops. How many are there?
Wow, ice cream! I like ice cream. Do you like ice cream?

facts.text: Ice cream is cold and sweet.
Ice cream can melt when it gets warm.
Ice cream comes in many colors and flavors.
You eat ice cream with a cone or a cup.
Ice cream can have sprinkles on top.

questions.text: How does ice cream taste?
What happens to ice cream in the sun?
How many scoops of ice cream are there?
What color is this ice cream?
Do you eat ice cream in a cone or a cup?
questions.hint: Sometimes a cone, sometimes a cup!

intro_templates: It's ice cream! Do you know how ice cream is made? It starts as a liquid and gets frozen!
Look at this ice cream! There are many flavors like chocolate, vanilla, and strawberry.
Here's some ice cream! Did you know it's made from milk and sugar?
Check out this ice cream! Some ice cream is soft and some is hard.
Meet this ice cream! It needs to stay in the freezer or it will melt.

facts.text: Ice cream is made by freezing a mixture of milk, sugar, and flavors.
There are many ice cream flavors like chocolate, vanilla, strawberry, and mint.
Ice cream needs to be kept in a freezer to stay solid and cold.
You can add toppings like sprinkles, chocolate sauce, or fruit to ice cream.
Ice cream cones are made from baked dough and are crunchy to eat.

questions.text: What are the main ingredients in ice cream?
questions.hint: It comes from cows and is sweet!

questions.text: Name three flavors of ice cream.
questions.hint: Think of colors: brown, white, pink...

questions.text: Where should we keep ice cream so it doesn't melt?
questions.hint: The very cold part of the fridge!

questions.text: What are some toppings you can put on ice cream?
questions.hint: Small colorful dots, or brown sauce...

questions.text: What is an ice cream cone made from?

intro_templates: It's ice cream! Did you know that ice cream needs air mixed in to make it soft and creamy?
Look at this ice cream! Long ago, people in China made a frozen treat from milk and rice.
Here's some ice cream! When it melts, it turns from solid back to liquid because of heat.
Check out this ice cream! Some people cannot eat regular ice cream, so they eat dairy-free kinds.
Think about ice cream. If we put it in the sun, it melts faster than in the shade.

facts.text: When making ice cream, air is mixed in to make it light and creamy, not icy.
Ice cream melts because heat from the air makes it change from solid to liquid.
Long ago, people in China mixed milk and rice and froze it to make a treat like ice cream.
Some people eat dairy-free ice cream made from almond milk or coconut milk.
If you leave ice cream out, it will melt and then refreezing it makes it icy and hard.

questions.text: Why is air mixed into ice cream when it's made?
questions.hint: It makes it soft, not hard like ice!

questions.text: Why does ice cream melt on a hot day?
questions.hint: Heat changes it from solid to liquid.

questions.text: Where did an early form of ice cream come from long ago?
What can people who cannot drink cow's milk eat instead of regular ice cream?
questions.hint: Ice cream made from nuts or coconuts!

questions.text: What happens if melted ice cream is put back in the freezer?

visual_features.size: varies (from small cherry to large beefsteak)

intro_templates: Look, a tomato! It's red and round.
This is a tomato. It grows on a plant.
A ripe tomato! It is juicy inside.
Hello, tomato! We can eat it raw or cooked.
Yummy tomato! It is good in salads.

facts.text: Tomatoes are usually red and round.
Tomatoes grow on plants with green leaves.
Tomatoes have many small seeds inside.
Tomatoes can be eaten raw or cooked.
Tomatoes are juicy when you bite them.

questions.text: What color is a ripe tomato?
How many tomatoes do you see?
Where do tomatoes grow?
Can you eat a tomato raw?
What do you find inside a tomato?

intro_templates: It's a tomato! Did you know it's actually a fruit, not a vegetable?
Look at this tomato! It's full of vitamins that are good for your body.
Here's a tomato! You can make many foods with it, like ketchup and sauce.
Check out this tomato! There are many different types, from small cherry tomatoes to big ones.
Meet this tomato! It needs sun and water to grow big and red.

facts.text: Tomatoes are actually fruits because they have seeds inside, but we often call them vegetables.
Tomatoes have Vitamin C, which helps keep you healthy and strong.
Tomatoes can be made into ketchup, pasta sauce, and tomato soup.
Cherry tomatoes are small and round, while beefsteak tomatoes are large and juicy.
Tomatoes need plenty of sunlight and water to grow from a small seed to a ripe fruit.

questions.text: Why is a tomato considered a fruit?
questions.hint: Think about what makes a fruit a fruit!

questions.text: What vitamin in tomatoes helps keep you healthy?

questions.hint: What do you put on fries?

questions.text: What is the difference between cherry tomatoes and beefsteak tomatoes?

intro_templates: It's a tomato! Did you know tomatoes were once thought to be poisonous?
Look at this tomato! Its seeds are spread by animals that eat the fruit.
Here's a tomato! The green parts of the plant are not safe to eat, but the ripe fruit is.
Check out this tomato! It changes from green to red as it ripens in the sun.
Consider this tomato! If we didn't have bees, tomatoes might not grow as well.

facts.text: Long ago, people in Europe thought tomatoes were poisonous because they are related to some toxic plants.
Animals eat tomatoes and spread the seeds in their droppings, helping new tomato plants grow.
The leaves and stems of tomato plants are not good to eat, but the ripe fruit is safe and healthy.
Tomatoes change color from green to red as they ripen because of a natural process in the sun.
Bees help tomato plants make fruit by moving pollen from one flower to another.

questions.text: Why did people long ago think tomatoes were poisonous?
questions.hint: They are related to some plants that are not safe.

questions.text: How do animals help spread tomato seeds?
questions.hint: They eat the fruit and then...!

questions.text: Which part of the tomato plant is safe to eat?
Why do tomatoes turn from green to red?
questions.hint: It happens when they get enough sunlight.

questions.text: What would happen if there were no bees to visit tomato flowers?
questions.hint: Think about how plants make fruit.

intro_templates: It's corn! Corn has yellow kernels in neat rows.
Look, corn! It's a tasty vegetable. What color is it?
Corn on the cob! The kernels are arranged in straight lines.
Hello, corn! You can eat it cooked. Do you like corn?
Yummy corn! It grows on a tall plant with green leaves.

facts.text: Corn kernels are arranged in straight rows on a cob.
Most corn kernels are yellow or white in color.
Corn grows on tall plants with long green leaves.
We eat the yellow kernels, not the green leaves outside.
Corn on the cob is cooked and eaten hot or cold.

questions.text: What color are most corn kernels?
How are the kernels arranged on a corn cob?
What part of the corn do we eat?
Where does corn grow?
Do we eat the green leaves of corn?

intro_templates: It's corn! Did you know corn can be popped to make popcorn?
Look at this corn! Corn is a type of grain, like rice and wheat.
Here's some corn! The silky threads on top help the corn grow.
Check out this corn! It can be yellow, white, or even two colors!
Meet corn! People have been eating corn for thousands of years.

facts.text: Some types of corn can pop when heated, turning into fluffy popcorn.
Corn is a grain, just like rice and wheat, and gives us energy.
The silky threads at the top of the corn catch pollen to help make kernels.
Corn can be eaten in many ways: boiled, grilled, or as cornflakes.
A single corn plant can grow one or two cobs of corn.

questions.text: What yummy snack can we make from some types of corn?
Is corn a vegetable or a grain?
What are the silky threads on top of the corn for?
questions.hint: They help the corn grow kernels!

questions.text: Name two ways we can eat corn.
How many cobs of corn can one plant grow?

intro_templates: It's corn! Corn is so useful that it's used to make many things, not just food.
Look at this corn! It needs a lot of sunlight and water to grow tall and healthy.
This is corn. The kernels we eat are actually the seeds of the corn plant.
Here we have corn. It's one of the most important crops grown around the world.
Consider corn. If we didn't have corn, we wouldn't have popcorn, corn syrup, or even some animal feed!

facts.text: Corn kernels are the seeds of the corn plant. If planted, they can grow into new corn plants.
Corn needs plenty of sunshine, water, and good soil to grow from a seed to a tall plant.
Corn is used to make many products like corn syrup, animal feed, and even some types of fuel.
Popcorn pops because each kernel has a tiny bit of water inside that turns to steam and makes it explode.
Corn is grown on every continent except Antarctica because it's too cold there for corn to grow.

questions.text: What can a corn kernel grow into if we plant it in the soil?
What three things does corn need to grow well?
Name one thing besides food that is made from corn.
questions.expected_answer: corn syrup, animal feed, fuel, etc.

questions.text: Why does popcorn pop when we heat it?
Is corn grown in Antarctica? Why or why not?

intro_templates: It's a dumpling! Dumplings are small and yummy!
Look, a dumpling! It has soft skin and tasty filling inside.
A hot dumpling! Dumplings are round or half-moon shaped.
Hello, dumpling! People eat dumplings with chopsticks or a fork.
Yum, a dumpling! It is warm and smells good.

facts.text: Dumplings have soft, white skin made from dough.
Dumplings have tasty meat or vegetables inside.
Dumplings are small and round, like a little pouch.
Dumplings are hot and steamy when cooked.
People eat dumplings with soy sauce for extra flavor.

questions.text: What color is the dumpling skin?
How many dumplings are on this plate?
Is the dumpling hot or cold when we eat it?
What is inside the dumpling?
What shape is a dumpling?

intro_templates: It's a dumpling! Did you know dumplings are a traditional food in many countries?
Look at this dumpling! It can be cooked in different ways - steamed, boiled, or fried!
Here's a dumpling! The skin is made from flour and water, and the filling can be many things.
Check out this dumpling! In China, people often eat dumplings during the New Year for good luck.
Meet this dumpling! Making dumplings is a fun family activity where everyone helps fold them.

facts.text: Dumplings can be cooked in three main ways: steamed, boiled, or pan-fried.
The dumpling wrapper is made by mixing flour and water to make dough.
In China, families eat dumplings during Lunar New Year for good fortune.
Dumpling fillings can be pork, chicken, shrimp, or vegetables like cabbage.
Fried dumplings have a crispy golden bottom that is crunchy to bite.

questions.text: What are the three ways to cook dumplings?
questions.hint: Think about using water, steam, or oil!

questions.text: What two main ingredients make the dumpling skin?
questions.hint: They are mixed to make dough!

questions.text: When do many Chinese families eat dumplings for good luck?
questions.hint: A big holiday in winter or spring!

questions.text: What makes fried dumplings different from boiled ones?
questions.hint: One is crunchy, one is soft!

questions.text: Name one kind of meat that can be in dumplings.

intro_templates: It's a dumpling! The pleats on the edge are not just pretty; they help seal the filling inside tightly.
Look at this dumpling! Why do you think dumplings are shaped like little pouches? To hold the filling!
This is a dumpling. Have you thought about why dumplings are eaten during celebrations? They symbolize wealth.
Here we have a dumpling. The dough wrapper protects the filling and keeps it juicy when cooked.
Consider this dumpling. If we didn't seal the edges well, what might happen when we boil it?

facts.text: The pleated edges of a dumpling help seal the filling inside so it doesn't come out when cooking.
Dumplings are shaped like ancient Chinese gold ingots, so they symbolize wealth and good luck.
The dough wrapper protects the juicy filling and keeps it moist during the cooking process.
If you don't seal the dumpling edges well, the filling might leak out into the cooking water.
Different countries have their own versions of dumplings, like Italian ravioli or Polish pierogi.

questions.text: Why do dumplings have pleated or folded edges?
questions.hint: To keep the inside from coming out!

questions.text: What do dumplings symbolize in Chinese culture?
What might happen if you forget to seal the dumpling edges before boiling?
Why is the dough wrapper important for the filling?
Can you name another country's version of dumplings?
